subject: update release scripts for gui apex dependency
commit_message:
update release scripts for gui apex dependency

policy-gui nas a dependency on apex-pdp that was missed in the earlier
versions of the release scripts.

Issue-ID: POLICY-3982
Change-Id: Ic729e2a315c7035e47a62255a375058a536ecf9a
Signed-off-by: liamfallon <liam.fallon@est.tech>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-parent-master-verify-java/858/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-parent-master-verify-java/858

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

@Liam, somewhat unrelated item with regards to releasing policy/docker each release that we do, should they be optional? reason being is that the docker images being based in openjdk or integration changes may have changes underneath that can cause some hidden problems.    I think we could release them during main release phases, but not past m4 unless there is a bug and we need to.   Note that code changes in our policy/docker base images are infrequent.   In the past, we didn't release them as much when it was manual.  Let me know what you think.

> @Liam, somewhat unrelated item with regards to releasing policy/docker each release that we do, should they be optional? reason being is that the docker images being based in openjdk or integration changes may have changes underneath that can cause some hidden problems.    I think we could release them during main release phases, but not past m4 unless there is a bug and we need to.   Note that code changes in our policy/docker base images are infrequent.   In the past, we didn't release them as much when it was manual.  Let me know what you think.

@Jorge Agree with you. One problem is that, if there are any fixes in the db-migrator, then we will have to release policy-docker in order to release the db-migrato.

Good point @Ajith about the db-migrator.   I think in the release file we can just have the containers that we are releasing, ie. policy-db-migrator and not the other ones, but don't want to complicate the  tooling much.
